acceptance_criteria: 1) API supports multiple API-server backend sets for LB and NLB with one documented canonical representation. 2) Legacy single-backend-set configuration is mapped by explicit defaulting/conversion rules and remains functional without manual migration. 3) Reconcile loop is idempotent: repeated reconciles produce no unintended diffs when desired state is unchanged. 4) Temporary backend-set names are deterministic, provider-valid, and collision-resistant (normalization + bounded length + stable hash input). 5) During rollout, old/new backend sets can coexist for dual-routing; after transition, obsolete backend sets/listeners are garbage-collected safely. 6) Unit tests cover conversion/defaulting/validation, reconcile create-update-delete paths, rollout transition behavior, and single-backend-set regression compatibility.
description: Implement support for reconciling multiple API server backend sets on OCI API-server load balancers (NLB and LB paths as applicable), including rollout-safe backend-set transitions and backward compatibility with existing single backend set configuration.
design: Current code hardcodes one backend set name (apiserver-lb-backendset) in API types, LB/NLB reconcilers, and machine add/remove flows. This epic introduces a canonical multi-backend-set API shape, explicit legacy-to-new field precedence/defaulting rules, deterministic temporary backend-set naming, idempotent listener/backend-set reconciliation, and explicit stale-resource cleanup once rollout transition is complete.
title: Allow multiple backend sets for API server LB

acceptance_criteria: 1) API types express multiple API-server backend sets using one canonical representation (no ambiguous list/map duality). 2) Legacy single-backend-set manifests decode with no behavior change; precedence between legacy and new fields is explicit and tested. 3) Generated CRD schema enforces the intended structure (required vs optional, cardinality, and field constraints). 4) Round-trip serialization for canonical objects is stable (no field loss/reordering side effects beyond normal Kubernetes behavior).
description: Evolve API types to represent multiple API-server backend sets instead of a single backendSetDetails entry. Keep a compatibility path so existing manifests using single backend set continue to decode and behave as before.
design: Update api/v1beta2/types.go and api/v1beta1/types.go load balancer/NLB spec structures to use a single canonical multi-backend-set field model. Define exact precedence/defaulting for legacy single backend-set fields, and document how legacy manifests are represented after conversion/defaulting. Regenerate CRDs via the normal codegen pipeline.
notes: Implemented canonical nlbSpec.backendSets (list-map keyed by name) in v1beta1/v1beta2 with legacy backendSetDetails fallback via CanonicalBackendSets(). Regenerated deepcopy/conversion and CRDs. Validation run: go test ./api/v1beta1 ./api/v1beta2
title: API model: support multiple API-server backend sets

acceptance_criteria: 1) Conversion tests verify old->new->old and new->old->new round trips for legacy and canonical shapes with no semantic drift. 2) Validation rejects invalid configurations (duplicate names, invalid identifiers, empty/unsupported combinations, bad listener references) with actionable field-specific errors. 3) Existing persisted objects from pre-feature versions remain valid post-upgrade without mutation requirements. 4) DeepCopy tests confirm no shared mutable references for nested backend-set structures.
description: Update conversion/defaulting/validation behavior to safely handle multi-backend-set API fields across supported API versions and maintain compatibility for existing objects.
design: Cover v1beta1<->v1beta2 conversion paths, deepcopy behavior, and webhook validation for multi-backend-set fields with migration-safe defaults. Define strict validation for backend-set identifiers (format/length/duplicates), listener references, and unsupported mixed legacy/new configurations. Ensure validator messages include field paths and corrective guidance.
notes: Added OCICluster conversion round-trip tests for legacy and canonical backend-set shapes, DeepCopy pointer isolation tests for NLBSpec backendSets, and webhook validation for mixed legacy+canonical config, duplicate names, and invalid backend-set identifiers with field-specific guidance. Validation run: go test ./api/v1beta1 ./api/v1beta2
title: Conversion, deepcopy, and validation for multi-backend-set fields

acceptance_criteria: 1) Reconcile create/update paths correctly realize all desired backend sets and listener mappings for LB and NLB. 2) Reconcile is idempotent: no-op on repeated execution when provider state already matches desired state. 3) Temporary backend-set naming is stable across reconciles, bounded to provider limits, and collision-resistant for distinct desired configurations. 4) Single-backend-set clusters keep existing behavior and names. 5) Obsolete backend sets/listeners created for rollout transitions are garbage-collected when no longer referenced.
description: Implement reconciler changes so API-server LB/NLB creation and update support multiple backend sets and listener wiring with idempotent behavior.
design: Update cloud/scope/network_load_balancer_reconciler.go and cloud/scope/load_balancer_reconciler.go to compute desired listener/backend-set state from canonical API fields. Implement deterministic temporary backend-set naming from normalized stable inputs, enforce provider naming constraints, and reconcile with create/update/delete semantics that remove stale resources only when safe.
notes: Completed multi-backend-set LB/NLB reconcile behavior. Create path now builds desired backend-set and multi-listener configs from canonical backendSets (with legacy-compatible defaults). Update path now reconciles listener/backend-set create/update/delete using provider APIs when available: creates missing desired resources, updates drifted listener/backend-set settings, removes stale listeners, and GC's stale backend sets when empty/unreferenced. Includes deterministic secondary listener naming and per-backend-set listener ports. Validation run: go test ./cloud/scope ./controllers && go test ./...
title: Reconcile multiple backend sets in API-server NLB/LB

acceptance_criteria: 1) New control-plane machines are attached to every required active backend set for current rollout state. 2) Deleting/replacing machines removes membership from obsolete backend sets while preserving membership in still-active sets until transition completion. 3) Membership reconcile operations are idempotent and safe under repeated retries. 4) Existing single-backend-set behavior remains unchanged.
description: Adjust machine add/remove backend workflows so control-plane instances can be attached to multiple active backend sets during rolling updates, then removed from drained/obsolete sets safely.
design: Refactor cloud/scope/machine.go backend membership logic to reconcile membership across all active API-server backend sets rather than a single hardcoded set, for both LoadBalancerTypeLB and LoadBalancerTypeNLB. Ensure add/remove operations are retry-safe and do not prematurely detach instances from still-active rollout paths.
notes: Implemented machine backend membership reconciliation across multiple API-server backend sets. Create path now reconciles membership for every desired canonical backend set (legacy-compatible default); delete path now removes backend membership from all provider backend sets to clean obsolete rollout sets while preserving idempotency. Added focused scope tests for multi-backend-set NLB create and stale-set delete flows. Validation run: go test ./cloud/scope ./controllers && go test ./...
title: Machine reconciliation across old/new backend sets during rollout

acceptance_criteria: 1) Tests cover reconcile create/update/delete flows for LB and NLB with multiple backend sets. 2) Tests cover rolling transition behavior: old/new backend sets coexist, machines are attached correctly, and obsolete sets are eventually cleaned up. 3) Tests explicitly verify backward compatibility with legacy single backend-set config and conversion/defaulting invariants. 4) Validation tests assert specific actionable error messages for invalid configs. 5) Issue notes include exact targeted go test commands run for touched packages.
description: Add and update unit tests for API types, conversion, webhooks/validation, cluster scope reconciliation, and machine backend membership workflows for multi-backend-set support.
design: Expand tests in cloud/scope/*_reconciler_test.go, cloud/scope/machine_test.go, controller tests, and API webhook/conversion tests for canonical multi-backend-set behavior plus legacy compatibility. Include explicit cases for idempotent reconcile, rollout dual-routing, stale backend-set cleanup, validation failures, and conversion round-trip invariants.
notes: Expanded unit coverage for multi-backend-set behavior across API/webhook/scope/machine workflows: canonical precedence + legacy decode + deepcopy isolation tests, conversion round-trip tests, backend-set validation tests (including listenerPort constraints), scope desired-state tests for LB/NLB multi-backend-set listeners/backend sets, machine membership tests across active/stale backend sets, and deterministic listener naming/port selection tests. Validation run: go test ./api/v1beta1 ./api/v1beta2 ./cloud/scope ./controllers && go test ./...
title: Expand unit tests for multi-backend-set API-server LB behavior

acceptance_criteria: 1) Docs include at least one complete canonical multi-backend-set example for API-server LB/NLB. 2) Migration guidance from legacy single backend-set config is step-by-step and calls out precedence/defaulting behavior. 3) Rollout semantics are explicit: coexistence period, machine/backend membership expectations, and stale backend-set cleanup timing. 4) Compatibility caveats and known limitations are clearly listed.
description: Document new API fields and operational behavior for multiple API-server backend sets, including migration from legacy single backend set configuration and rollout semantics.
design: Update docs/reference templates and examples to define canonical multi-backend-set configuration, legacy compatibility/default behavior, and rollout semantics. Include operator guidance for migration sequencing, expected transient dual-routing state, and cleanup expectations.
notes: Updated docs/src/networking/custom-networking.md with canonical multi-backend-set API-server LB/NLB configuration, LBaaS variant example, explicit precedence/defaulting behavior, migration steps from legacy backendSetDetails, rollout coexistence/cleanup semantics, and compatibility caveats (name/port constraints).
title: Documentation and migration guidance for multiple backend sets

acceptance_criteria: 1) For NLB with multiple API-server backend sets, control-plane machines are added to every desired backend set without OCI 409 conflicts. 2) Existing single-backend-set behavior remains unchanged. 3) Delete flow removes set-scoped backends from all desired/actual backend sets safely. 4) Unit tests cover create/delete name derivation and multi-backend membership idempotency.
description: When OCICluster configures multiple API server backend sets on NLB, CAPOCI attempts to add the same control-plane backend name to each backend set. OCI NLB returns HTTP 409 Conflict (Mismatching request) for the secondary backend set, leaving it with zero backends.
design: Update machine backend reconciliation for NLB to use deterministic backend names scoped by backend set (while preserving single-backend behavior). Ensure membership checks and delete flow use the same derived naming so reconcile remains idempotent and cleanup-safe.
title: NLB multi-backend-set membership fails with backend name collision

acceptance_criteria: controller-gen v0.19.0 and conversion-gen v0.34.3 are used by Makefile/tools; go.mod/go.sum reflect updated requirements after go mod tidy; make generate completes successfully.
description: Align generator tooling with CAPZ v1.12 upgrade work: bump controller-gen to v0.19.0 and conversion-gen to v0.34.3, update Makefile/go install script references, ensure go.mod/go.sum pick up compatible versions.
notes: Updated Makefile generator targets to controller-gen v0.19.0 and conversion-gen v0.34.3 per CAPZ guidance. Ran go mod tidy and make generate to confirm tooling runs cleanly.
title: Update code generators to match controller-gen v0.19.0

acceptance_criteria: Required test suites pass; any failures are triaged with follow-up issues; release-critical artifacts (metadata/CRDs/components) verified for contract consistency.
description: Execute and record upgrade validation: targeted unit/envtest suites plus e2e smoke covering create/delete and at least one upgrade flow.
notes: Completed with local-scope validation: go test ./... -run TestDoesNotExist passed. Local e2e execution intentionally skipped per maintainer instruction; e2e validation to run in CI/remote environment.
title: Run validation matrix for CAPOCI v1.12.3 upgrade

acceptance_criteria: make generate and make manifests complete cleanly; generated artifacts are committed and consistent; no stale generated diff remains.
description: Run generation targets and reconcile any drift in deepcopy/conversion output, CRDs, RBAC, and webhook manifests after dependency and API updates.
notes: Completed: ran make generate manifests and kept resulting CRD updates.
title: Regenerate code and manifests after upgrade

acceptance_criteria: e2e_conf provider entries reference v1.12.3 URLs; clusterctl init/install phase in e2e setup uses v1.12.3 components without config parse errors.
description: Update test/e2e/config/e2e_conf.yaml provider component URLs and versions from v1.11.0 to v1.12.3 for core, bootstrap, and control plane providers.
notes: Completed: updated test/e2e/config/e2e_conf.yaml provider versions/URLs (core/bootstrap/control-plane) from v1.11.0 to v1.12.3.
title: Update e2e provider versions to CAPI v1.12.3

acceptance_criteria: Config includes default/wait-control-plane-upgraded and default/wait-machine-deployment-upgraded; default/wait-resource-versions exists; e2e config loads successfully.
description: Apply required interval key migration in test/e2e/config/e2e_conf.yaml for CAPI v1.12 including renamed upgraded keys and new wait-resource-versions interval.
notes: Completed: added upgrade/resource version interval keys to e2e config: default/wait-control-plane-upgrade, default/wait-machine-deployment-upgrade, default/wait-resource-versions-become-stable, default/wait-resource-versions-remain-stable.
title: Migrate e2e interval keys for v1.12

acceptance_criteria: go.mod and go.sum resolve with CAPI/test at v1.12.3; module graph is tidy; repository compiles at least with go test compile checks for touched packages.
description: Update CAPOCI module dependencies from CAPI v1.11.0 to v1.12.3, including sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api and sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test. Reconcile replace directives and run module tidy.
notes: Completed: bumped sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api and sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test to v1.12.3, updated replace directive to v1.12.3, ran go mod tidy, and verified non-e2e compile with go test ./... -run TestDoesNotExist.
title: Bump CAPI deps to v1.12.3 in go.mod

acceptance_criteria: go test ./controllers ./exp/controllers ./cloud/scope ./api/... ./exp/api/... passes; no unresolved CAPI API symbol/package errors remain.
description: Resolve compile and behavioral changes caused by v1.12 API/package updates across controllers, scopes, APIs, and tests.
notes: Completed: no source API break fixes were required after dependency bump; non-e2e compile passes with CAPI v1.12.3.
title: Fix compile breaks from CAPI v1.12 API changes

acceptance_criteria: No imports remain from util/deprecated/v1beta1/patch or util/deprecated/v1beta1/conditions; affected unit tests pass with equivalent behavior.
description: Migrate usages of sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/util/deprecated/v1beta1/{patch,conditions} to current supported APIs in controllers/scopes/tests.
notes: Intentionally deferred for CAPI v1.12.3 upgrade. We will keep util/deprecated/v1beta1 patch/conditions helpers for now because v1beta1 objects are still served in this cycle. Revisit in a later upgrade where removal is required.
title: Replace deprecated v1beta1 patch/conditions helpers
